frameworks.name: history-dependent perceptual bias
frameworks.one_line: Past stimuli, choices, and outcomes bias current perceptual decisions

frameworks.name: synaptic plasticity
frameworks.one_line: NMDA-dependent plasticity as candidate substrate of across-trial history effects

frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: Within-trial accumulation of fluctuating sensory evidence in a 2AFC task

frameworks.name: pharmacological perturbation
frameworks.one_line: NMDA blockade and GABA-A enhancement in healthy human participants

core_question: Do NMDA receptors causally support task-adaptive history-dependent biases in human perceptual decisions?
key_assumptions: History biases reflect a plasticity-based memory mechanism
Pharmacological agents selectively target NMDA vs GABA-A pathways
Within- and across-trial integration can be dissociated in behavior
key_findings: Choices were biased by within-trial accumulation of fluctuating sensory evidence
Choices were biased by across-trial accumulation of stimulus sequences, adapting to block statistics
NMDA receptor blockade reduced task-adaptive across-trial history-dependent biases
GABA-A enhancement increased transiency of within-trial evidence accumulation dynamics
interpretations: NMDA-receptor-dependent plasticity mediates adaptive across-trial history biases
Within-trial and across-trial integration rely on dissociable neurochemical mechanisms
